ety: for A. زندقة, v.n. of زندق, fr. the P. zandīk, fr. zand
def: s.m. A belief in the two principles of light and darkness; a bad religion; impiety, unbelief, heresy, atheism.

ety: = S. शङ्ख
def: s.m. A small bell (cf. zangūla).
more: n. m. 1. (H. jar ) Rust; canker. 2. A small bell (of brass). zang-ālūdah , adj. Rusty; covered with rust. zang lagnā , v. n. To become rusty.
s. m. 1. Rust. 2. A small bell. 3. The country of Zang-bār in Africa. زنگ لگنا zang lagnā, To become rusty. زنگ آلود zang-ālūd, adj. Rusty, covered with rust.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian زنگ (zang).

ety: old P. gang; Pehl. kang; Zend kaṅha; S. कृष्णं?
def: adj. Covered with rust, rusty
subs: s.m. Black ink; — the country called Zanguebar
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian زنگ (zang).

def: rusty
wety: زنگ (zaṅg, “rust”) + آلود (ālūd, “infected”), literally “rust infected”

def: s.m. = زنگار zangār, q.v. s.m. Verdigris; rust; (in comp.) acetate; sulphate, &c.
more: s. (dakh.) Rust, canker (of brass), verdigrise ( Harris ): see زنگار zangār.

def: adj. = زنگاري zangārī, q.v. adj. Rusty; rust-coloured; — colour of verdigris, green.

ety: zang, q.v.+Zend aff. ar, S. ara, or ra
def: s.m. Verdigris; rust; (in comp.) acetate; sulphate, &c.
more: n. m. Verdigris. zangār pitrai . Acetate of copper.

ety: zangār, q.v.+ī = S. इन्
def: adj. Rusty; rust-coloured; — colour of verdigris, green.
more: adj. Of the color of verdigris; green.
adj. Rusty, of the colour of rust.

def: s. f. (dakh.) Quarrelling, fighting.
s.m. See s.v. zang. s.m. A small bell (cf. zangūla).
more: s. Black ink: name of a country.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian زنگبار (zaṅgibār). By surface analysis, زَن٘گ (zaṅg) + بار (bār).

ety: zang, q.v.+ī = S. इन्
def: adj. & s.m. Of Ethiopia, Ethiop, Moor, negro; black; — an Ethiopian; a negro, a blackamoor
more: n. m. An Ethiop; a negro; Moor.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian زنگی (zangī).

ety: zang, 'bell,' q.v.+dim. aff. ūla or ula = S. ल or र
def: s.m. A little bell; small globular bells hung round the rim of a tambourine; a cymbal.
more: s. m. A little bell, the small bells on a tambourin, a cymbal.

ety: see zan, fr. zadan
def: s.f. Beating, striking, attacking, &c. (used in comp., e.g. lat-zanī, 'kicking'; rah-zanī, 'highway robbery').
more: (in compos. only) s. f. Striking, the act of striking.

ety: for A. زانٍ zāniṉ, act. part. of زني 'to commit adultery'
def: s.m. Adulterer, fornicator, whoremonger.
more: n. m. زنا he committed adultery. An adulterer; a fornicator.
(part. act. of زنا Heb. To commit adultery) s. m. An adulterer, a whoremonger.

ety: for A. زانية, fem. of zānī
def: s.f. An adulteress, a strumpet, harlot.
more: (part. act. of زنا) s. f. A whore, a strumpet, an adultress.

ety: S. शृङ्गवेर 'horn-shaped'; Gr. ζιγγίβερις; Lat. zingiber
def: s.f. Dry ginger.
more: n. f. S. शृङ्गवेर from शृङ्ग a horn, वेर a body, shaped like a horn. Dry ginger (H. soṅṭh ).
s. f. Dry ginger. (Sans. शृंगवेर comp. of शृंग A horn, and वेर A body; q. d. Shaped like a horn).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian زنجبیل (zanjabīl), from Arabic زَنْجَبِيل (zanjabīl).

ety: zan, q.v.+A. jalab, fr. جلب 'to drive,' &c.
def: s.m. A pander to his own wife, a cuckold.
s.f. Panderism to one's own wife, cuckoldom.

ety: = S. शृङ्खला
def: s.f. Chain; fetters (zanjīr, 'chain,' is applied to elephants, as rās, 'rein,' is applied to horses)
more: n. f. A chain. zanjīr-bandī , n. f. 1. A series; train. zanjīr-i-zāmnī , n. f. Chain or joint security; mutual or collateral surety or assurance.
subs: s.f. A chain for the neck in which stones are set at intervals.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian زنجیر (zanjir).

def: s.m. A necklace; — chain-stitch.
more: n. m. 1. A necklace. 2. Chain stitch.

def: direct plural of زَنْجِیر (zanjīr, “chain, shackle”)
adj. & s.m. Chained, in chains; mad, insane; — a prisoner; — a maniac.
more: adj. Chained, in chains; mad, a prisoner.

def: oblique plural of زَنْجِیر (zanjīr, “chain, shackle”)
vocative plural of زَنْجِیر (zanjīr, “chain, shackle”)

ety: zan, q.v.+ka = S. क
def: s.f. A little woman.
effeminate; impotent
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian زنخا (zanxā).

ety: prob. for P. zanka, q.v.
def: adj. & s.m. Effeminate; weak, impotent; — a eunuch; — a dancing-boy.
more: n. m. 1. A eunuch. 2. A dancing boy. zankhā , adj. 1. Effeminate. 2. Weak in the loins.

def: effeminacy
wety: زنخا (zanxā, “effeminate”) + ـپن (-pan).

def: uxorious; henpecked
wety: زَن (zan, “female, wife”) + مُرِید (murīd, “follower, disciple”).

ety: inf. n. of ظنّ 'to think, opine,' &c.
def: s.m. Thought, opinion, notion, idea, supposition, conjecture; suspicion, evil opinion; jealousy
more: n. m. ظن opined. 1. Impression; notion. 2. Conjecture ( bharam ). zann-i-gālib . Strong or violent presumption; great probability. zann-i-fāsid, gumān-i-bad . 1. An evil thought. 2. Suspicion. bad zan , adj. Suspicions; sceptical.

def: s.m. = sannāṭā, q.v. s.m. Loud or violent sound, rumbling noise, clatter (made by wind and rain or hail, &c. at a distance), howling (of the wind), roaring, roar (of waves, or a conflagration, &c.); violent blast or gust; a dashing or driving (of rain, &c.); ringing, whizzing, whiz (of bullets, &c.); vehemence, animation, briskness and eagerness; — a transport of passion or rage; — a howling wilderness, a dreary place or spot; a stunning blow or shock; a state of alarm or terror, consternation; amazement; anything monstrous or frightful; — adj. Enormous, huge, vast, monstrous; — still, silent, lonely, dreary, desolate, frightful
subs: (-ko), To receive a shock, to be shocked, be amazed, be terror-stricken, be stunned, to fall into a swoon
adj. (f. -ī), Violent, terrible, awful, fearful, monstrous, appalling, shocking; dreary, desolate
v.n. To fall into or continue in a state of alarm or terror, to be stricken motionless with terror or astonishment
v.n. To be or become motionless (with astonishment or fear, &c.), to be paralyzed (with fear, &c.).

def: n. m. The whiz of a solid body through the air.
(rel. n. fr. z̤ann), Thought, supposed; suspected.

ety: Pehl. shnuk, zānuk; Zend zhnu; S. जानु
def: s.m. The knee; the lap
more: n. m. Z. zānūk ; S. jānū . The thigh. zānū badalnā , v. n. To rest the knees alternately. du-zānū , adv. lit . sitting on one's thighs and heels. Kneeling. du-zānū baiṭhnā , v. n. To kneel.
(s. जानु ) s. m. The knee, the lap.
subs: v.n. To change the knees, to rest the knees alternately (in kneeling)
To sit on the hams, to kneel.

def: s. (dakh. i. q. آوازپر) Sound of a wing (?).

ety: fr. Zend zar = S. हृ (हृणीयते; and, in some senses, app. = S. हृ, हर्); cf. āzār
def: s.m. Groan, plaint, lamentation, wailing (cf. zārī); desire, wish; collection, multitude, crowd; (as a suffix) place where anything grows in abundance, place, bed, garden ( = stān or istān; e.g. gul-zār, 'a rose-garden') — adj. Groaning, lamenting, afflicted; thin, lean, weak
more: ( In comp .) Place; multitude, as gulzār a garden of roses, sabzah-zār a meadow. zār zār, zār nizār, zār qatār; Illit. zār be- zār . Great lamentation. zār zār ronā , v. n. To weep bitterly. Gale lag-ke rone lagī zār zār; Kīyā apne tan man ko us par nisār . Mīr Hasan. zār o nizār , adj. 1. Emaciated; lean. 2. Weak.

1. s. m. Desire, wish. 2. A groan, lamentation. 3. adj. Afflicted, groaning, lamenting. زار زار zār-zār, Great lamentation. زارنالي zār-nālī, Lamentation. زارو نزار zār-o-nizār, Emaciated. 4. (in compos.) It implies place, multitude; as, گلزار gul-zār, s. m. A garden of roses.
subs: and zār-qitār, s.m. Great lamentation; — adv. With great lamentation; piteously, bitterly, exceedingly, to excess (e.g. zār-zār ronā, 'to weep bitterly')
s.m. Great lamentation; — adj. Thin, lean, emaciated; weak, feeble.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian زار (zār), ultimately from Proto-Iranian *jāráyati.

ety: Zend zairi, rt. zar = S. हरि 'yellow'; but cf. Zend zaranya, 'gold'; and S. हिरण्य्
def: s.m. Gold; money, riches, wealth
more: n. m. 1. Gold. 2. Riches ( drab ). Zar hai to nar hai, nahīṅ panchhī be-par hai . Prov. If you 've wealth you are a man; if not, a wingless bird. zar-i-asl , n. m. A principal sum bearing interest. zar-i-amānat , n. m. Deposit money; trust fund. zar-i-āmdanī , n. m. Proceeds; receipts; income; profits; dividend; revenue. zar-i-bāqī, bāqī , n. m. Outstanding balance. zar-baft , n. m. Brocade; cloth of gold. zar-i-byā'na ; H. sāī , n. m. Earnest money. zar-parast , adj. 1. Venal; mercenary. 2. Selfish; sordid. zar-i-peshgī , n. m. Payment in advance. zar-i-tāvān ; H. ḍanḍ , n. m. Penalty; fine. zar-i-tahsīl , n. m. Collections; revenue. zar-i-jāfarī , n. m. Gold of the purest kind. zar-i-jurmānā . A fine. zar-i-kharchā . Costs of suit. zar-i-kharīd , Purchase money. zar-kharīd , adj. Purchased with one's own money. zar-khez, zar-rez , adj. lit . producing gold. Fertile (soil); rich (land).…
n. f. Nonsense; gibberish. ziṛ'ṛī , n. m. A talkative person ( bakvāsī ). ziṛ hāṅknā , v. a. To talk nonsense; chatter.

s. m. Gold, riches, wealth, money. زراندود zar-andūd or زراندوده zar-andūda, Gilt, plated with gold. زرپرست zar-parast, Venal, selfish. زرخريد zar-ḵẖarīd or زرخريده zar-ḵẖarīda, adj. Purchased (as, a slave). زرخيز zar-ḵẖez, adj. Rich (a country), producing gold and silver; fertile. زردار zar-dār, adj. Wealthy. زردوز zar-doz (comp. of زر and part. of دوختن To sew) 1. adj. Embroidered. 2. s. Embroiderer. زردوزي zar-doz-ī, s. f. Embroidery. زردوست zar-dost, adj. Fond of money, covetous. زرسرخ zar-i-surḵẖ, s. m. 1. Gold. 2. Pure gold of a red colour, gold coin. 3. Name of a suit in cards: see under تاج tāj. زرسفيد zar-i-sufaid, s. m. 1. Silver. 2. A suit in cards: see under تاج tāj. زرگر zar-gar, s. m. A goldsmith. زرگري zar-gar-ī, s. f. 1. The business of a goldsmith. 2. A kind of dialect. زرگل zar-i-gul, The yellow stamina of a rose. زرنقد zar-i-naḳd, Ready money, cash. زرنگار zar-nigār, a…
subs: s.m. A principal sum; pure gold
s.m. Income, revenue; receipts, proceeds, profits
part. adj. Covered or plated with gold, gilded, gilt
s.m. Brocade, gold tissue, cloth of gold ( = zar-bāft); — one who works in gold thread, a gold-lace worker
adj. Woven in gold, brocaded, embroidered
s.m. Remaining money, balance, outstanding balance
adj. & s.m. Venal, mercenary; selfish, sordid; — a mammonist; a sordid or selfish person
adj. Purchased with one's own money, purchased (as a slave)
adj. Producing gold; rich (a country); fertile
v.t. To enrich; to make productive, to fertilize
adj. & s.m. Wealthy, rich; — a moneyed man
s.f. Embroidery; — zar-dost, adj. & s.m. Fond of money, covetous; — one who is fond of money, a miser
s.m. 'Red gold,' pure gold; gold; gold coin; — name of a suit in cards (see s.v. tāj)
s.m. Silver; — a suit in cards (see tāj)
s.m. Security for money-payment, a deposit-security
s.m. Amount of a debt; debts
s.m. 'Gold shoe'; embroidered shoe; (met.) a douceur, a bribe
s.f. Beating gold into thin leaves; the business of a gold-beater

s.f. The business of a goldsmith; — a secret or enigmatical mode of speech (similar to what, in English, is called the p language, the letter z being used instead of p; as tuzum kazhāṅ jazātize huzo, i.e. tum kahāṅ jāte ho)
s.m. The yellow stamina of a rose
adj. Gilded, gilt; inlaid with gold
s.m. Money to be got in, balance to be received, debts due, claims, demands, outstandings.

ety: inf. n. of زرع 'to sow'
def: s.m. = zirāʻat, q.v. s.f. Sowing, tilling; tillage, husbandry, agriculture; a sown or cultivated field, sown land; a standing crop
more: s. m. Sowing, seed sown, a sown field.
subs: s.m. One whose business or occupation is sowing or tilling, husbandman, agriculturist
(-kī), To till, to cultivate; to grow crops; to reclaim (land).

def: = P زراتشت zarātusht, prop. n. = زردشت zardusht, q.v.

ety: for A. ظرافة, inf. n. of ظرف 'to be clever, or ingenious, or polite,' &c.
def: s.f. Beauty; ingenuity, &c. ( = z̤arf, q.v.); wit, humour, jocularity, facetiousness, pleasantry, fun, jest.
more: n. f. ظرف was ingenious. Wit; humour; facetiousness. zarāfatan, mazāqan ; H. haṅsī se , adj. Facetiously.
s. f. 1. Beauty. 2. Politeness, elegance, gallantry. 3. Wit, humour, jocularity, facetiousness.

ety: for A. ظرافةً, acc. of z̤arāfat
def: adv. Facetiously, in fun, by way of jest.
formal plural of ذَرِیعَہ (zariya)

ety: v.n. fr. ضرّ 'to harm,' &c.
def: s.m. Harm, injury, hurt, mischief, damage; defect, deficiency, detriment, loss; — poverty; affliction, distress, anguish
more: n. m. ضر hurt. 1. Injury; detriment; wrong ( choṭ , 1). 2. Damage ( zyān ). zarar pahuṅchānā , v. a. To injure; hurt. zarar-i-jismānī , G. G. n. m. Bodily hurt or injury. zarar-i-khafīf , n. m. Slight harm. zarar-rasānī , n. f. Causing injury, annoyance. zarar-i-shadīd , G. G. n. m. Grievous injury. zarar kā khatrah , n. m. Risk of harm. bil-irādah zarar pahuṅchānā , G. G. Voluntarily causing hurt.
s. m. 1. Injury, damage, detriment, loss, ruin. 2. Affliction, anguish.
subs: (-ko), To cause harm to come (to), to harm, hurt, &c.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian ضرر (zarar), from Arabic ضَرَر (ḍarar).

def: = P زرادشت zarādusht, prop. n. = زردشت zardusht, q.v.

ety: inf. n. of ضرب 'to beat, strike,' &c.
def: s.f. Beating, striking; — stamping; sealing; coining (money); a beating, blow, stroke (of a whip, &c.), wound (of a sword); a sprain, hurt, injury; loss, damage; — stamp, impression (on a coin, &c.); violence, vehemence, emphasis; (in Arith.) multiplying, multiplication; — making or constituting (a saying) proverbial; — a piece (as of cannon, &c.; e.g. ćahār ẓarb top, 'four pieces of cannon'; cf. rās, and zanjīr); — adj. & s.f. Beaten, struck; stamped, coined, &c.; — coined money, a coin
more: n. f. 1. A stroke; blow. 2. Stamp; impression; stamping (coin). 3. ( Math .) Multiplication. 4. Pieces of cannon. Chahār zarab top. zarab uṭhānā , v. n. To suffer loss or damage. zarb-i-shadīd , Grievous hurt. zarab-ul-masal ; H. kahāvat , n. f. A saying; common saying; saw; proverb. zarab-ul masal , adj. Notorious; proverbial; current ( zabāṅ-zad ). zarab-i-khafīf . G. G. A slight wound or blow. zarab denā, yā k .; H. gunā k. , v. a. ( Arith .) To multiply. zarab-i-shamshīr , n. f. A sword-wound or cut. zarab lagānā , v. a. 1. To give a blow; to strike; hammer. 2. To impress upon the mind. zarab murakkab ; H. milā gun , n. f. Compound multiplication. zarab-mufrad ; H. sādhāran-gun , n. f. Simple multiplication. zarab-i-mohlik , n. f. A mortal wound. āla-i-zarab-i-sikka . A coining instrument. hāsil-zarab ; H. phal , n. m. ( Arith .) The product. dār-ul-zarab ; H. ṭaksāl , n. m. The mint…

s. f. 1. A blow, beating. 2. Violence. 3. Multiplication (in arithmetick). 4. Striking, emphasis. 5. Stamping, coining (money), struck, coined. ضرب دينا ẓarb denā, To coin. ضرب المثل ẓarbu-l-mas̤al or ضرب مثل ẓarb-i-mas̤al, s. f. A proverb. دارالضرب dāru-ẓ-ẓarb, s. f. The mint-office.
subs: v.n. To suffer injury, or damage, or loss
(-ko), To sustain an injury, to get hurt, &c.
(-ko), To stamp, coin; — to multiply (one number into, -meṅ, another)
v.t. To multiply ( = ẓarb denā, q.v.)
v.t. To strike, give (one) a blow; to hammer; — to stamp or impress (upon, -meṅ)
or ẓarb-ě-mas̤al, adj. & s.f. Constituted, or passed into, a proverb; proverbial; current; notorious; a form of saying, a common saying, a proverb, saw
or ẓarb-ḵẖāna, s.m. 'The stamping- or coining- house, the mint.

def: s.m. = P ضربت ẓarbat, s.f. (for A. ضربة, n. of un. fr. ẓarb, q.v.), One blow, a blow, a stroke, &c.
For this and other compounds beginning with zar which are not given below, see s.v. زر zar.
more: (زر Gold, and part. of بافتن To weave) s. Brocade, gold-tissue.

def: adj. Woven in gold, embroidered.
(زر Gold, and بفت from بافتن To weave) s. m. Brocade, cloth of gold.
s.f. = P ضربه ẓarba, s.m. (for A. ضربة, n. of un. fr. ẓarb, q.v.), One blow, a blow, a stroke, &c.
more: (noun of unity of ضرب) s. f. A blow.

def: s.f. pl. (of ẓarbat), Blows; strokes, &c.

ety: Pehl. zart; Zend zairit, rt. zar; S. हरित, rt. हृ
def: adj. Yellow (syn. pīlā); pale, pallid, wan, livid
more: adj. Yellow ( pīlā ); wan; livid. zard-ālū, zard-āṛū , n. m. An apricot. zard-āb , n. m. Serum. zard paṛnā yā ho jānā , v. n. To turn pale. zard pīlak , n. m. Oriolus kundu ; the golden oriole. It has a sweet song and comes in usually with the mango blossoms. It is known as the 'mango bird.' zard chob , n. f. lit . yellow wood. Turmeric; curcuma longa. zard rag, rag-i-jān , ( Anat .) Arteries. zard-rū , adj. Bashful; ashamed.

adj. Yellow, pale, wan, livid. زرد پيلک zard-pīlak, s. m. The name of a bird. زرد رنگ zard-rang, adj. 1. Yellow. 2. ( met. ) Bashful. زرد رو zard-rū, adj. Bashful, ashamed. زرد روٴي zard-rū-ī, s. f. Bashfulness. زرد گوش zard-gosh, adj. Hypocritical, malignant.
subs: s.m. 'Yellow water'; bile; serum; pus or matter running from a wound
s.m. The golden oriole, Oriolus kundu (commonly called the 'mango-bird,' because it arrives when the mango-tree blossoms)
adj. Yellow; (met.) bashful; s.m. The colour yellow
adj. & s.m. Hypocritical; malignant; — a hypocrite; a flatterer
v.n. To become pale or livid.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian زرد (zard).

ety: fr. zard, q.v.
def: s.m. A sweet dish made of rice, raisins and almonds cooked in spices and coloured with saffron (a kind of pulāʼo, but without meat)
more: n. m. 1. Tobacco for chewing ( tambākū ); a quid. W. Maṅgvāyā zardah, chhālyā, tere ishq ne mujhe khā līyā . Now for tobacco, now for betel-nut she makes me run, This love for her is killing work, my life from me 's clean gone. 2. A kind of sweet pulāo , dressed with saffron. parde meṅ zardah lagānā , To commit adultery secretly. Woh to parde meṅ zardah lagātī haiṅ . Mah.
s. m. A kind of pulāʼo, dressed with saffron.
subs: (eaten with pān, or chewed alone; — cf. sūkhā); — the yolk of an egg (more commonly zardī, q.v.).

ety: zard, q.v.+aff. ak = S. क; or = zarda
def: adj. & s.f. Yellowish; — a carrot.
s. m. An apricot.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian زردآلو (zard-ālū, literally “yellow plum”). Compare Gujarati જરદાળુ (jardāḷu), Kannada ಜರ್ದಾಳು (jardāḷu), Marathi जर्दाळू (jardāḷū), Punjabi ਜਰਦਾਲੂ (jardālū).

def: s. f. Yellow wood, turmerick. (Curcuma longa).

ety: zard, q.v.+Zend aff. ī = S. इ
def: s.f. Yellowness; yellow colour; paleness; jaundice; — yolk of an egg; — a gold mohur
more: n. f. 1. A yellow color; paleness. 2. The yolk of an egg. 3. The jaundice. 4. Yellow boys; a gold mohar. zardī chhānā , v. n. To turn pale.
s. f. 1. Yellowness. 2. The yolk of an egg.
subs: (-par), Paleness to come (over the face), to become pale.

def: gold embroidery
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian زردوزی (zardōzī).

ety: Pehl. zartusht; Zend zarathustra
def: prop. n. Zoroaster, founder of the religion of the Magi.
more: n. prop. Zoroaster, founder of the ancient religion of Persia.

ety: inf. n. of ظرف; see z̤arāfat
def: s.m. Ingenuity, skill, cleverness; beauty, excellence; elegance (of manners), politeness ( = z̤arāfat); — capacity, capability; a receptacle, vessel, vase; (in Gram.) an adverb (of time or place); — adj. Ingenious, skilful, clever; beautiful, graceful; elegant, polite
more: n. m. pl. zurūf , 1. A vessel ( bartan ). 2. Capacity; capability ( samāī ). 3. ( Gram .) An adverb of time and place. zarf-i-zamāṅ . Adverb of time. zarf-i-makāṅ . Adverb of place. ālī-zarf . Large-minded. kam-zarf, tunak-zarf . Shallow ( ochhā 1). zurūf-i-chīnī . China; porcelain. zurūf-i-nuqraī . Silver plates, spoons, etc.

s. m. 1. Ingenuity, elegance, politeness. 2. A vase, vessel; capability. 3. adj. Ingenious. 4. (in grammar) An adverb. ظرف زمان z̤arf-i-zamān, Adverb of time. ظرف مکان z̤arf-i-makān, Adverb of place. (Plur. ظروف z̤urūf, Vessels; adverbs, &c.)
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian ظَرْف (zarf), from Arabic ظَرْف (ẓarf).

ety: zar, q.v.+Zend aff. i = S. इ
def: s.f. Anything woven with gold thread; gold lace; gold brocade
more: n. f. Any thing woven with gold thread. Zarī gotā purānā! Delhi Street cry. Any old gold lace to sell! zarī-bāf , n. m. A manufacturer in gold thread; a gold-lace worker. zar'rīṅ , adj. Golden ( sunahrī ).
(from زر) s. f. Any thing woven with gold thread. زري باف zarī-bāf, A gold-lace worker.
subs: s.m. A manufacture in gold thread; a gold-lace worker
s.f. The business or trade of gold-lace working

ety: zār, q.v.+Zend aff. i = S. इ
def: s.f. Weeping, sighing, groaning, wailing, lamentation, begging and praying, supplication, entreaty, crying (or cry) for help.
more: n. f. Weeping and wailing; lamentation. Din kaṭā faryād se aur rāt zārī se kaṭī, Umr kaṭne ko kaṭī par kyā hi khuārī se kaṭī ! My days passed in complainings, and my nights in weepings vain, The measure of my days filled up, but filled, ah! with what pain!
s. f. Crying, sighing, groaning, lamentation, supplication, crying for help.

r: ẕarīʻa, ẕariʻa, ẕarīʻat, ẕirāʻ, orig, wasīla, ẕarīʻa paidā karnā, ẕarīʻa rakhnā, ẕarīʻe, ẕarīʻe-se, ba-ẕarīʻa, ke, is ẕarīʻe-se
def: (for A. ذريعة ẕarīʻat, fr. ذرع 'to stretch forth the arm'; cf. ẕirāʻ), s.m. orig. 'A cover or screen (as a beast, &c.) behind which a hunter lurks and approaches game'; (hence) a means of access or approach, medium, means, instrumentality, agency, cause, occasion, interest, favour, ingratiation, intervention, intercession (syn. wasīla)
more: n. m. ذرع stretching forth. 1. Medium; means, instrument; instrumentality; agency. 2. Influence; interest; favor. zariya paidā k. , v. n. To make interest. zariya rakhnā , v. n. To have interest. zariye se, ba-zariye , adv. In or by virtue of; through the intervention or instrumentality of; under cover; thereby; hereby. is zariye se , G. G. ( Law ) By these presents. ba-zariye tār-barqī , By telegram. ba-zariye ḍāk , By post. ba-zariye ohdeh-dārī, Ex-officio ; in virtue of one's office.
subs: To find means; to make interest
postpn., and ba-ẕarīʻa, prep., By means (of, -ke), by, by the medium (of), through the instrumentality or the intervention (of); in or by virtue (of); under cover (of)
By this means; hereby; by these presents.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian ذَرِیعَه (zarī'a), from Arabic ذَرِيعَة (ḏarīʕa, “pretext”).

ety: act. part. of ضرب 'to beat,' &c.
def: part. adj. Beating, bruising, striking; stamping, coining.
more: part. act. (of ضرب) Beating, bruising, striking, stamping, coining.

ety: v.n. fr. ظرف; see z̤arāfat
def: adj. & s.m. Clever, ingenious; elegant, polite, gallant; excellent, good; — witty, facetious, jocose, arch, comical, waggish; — a wit; a wag.
more: adj. Witty ( ṭhaṭol ). zarīf , n. m. A wit ( ṭhaṭol ).
(from ظرف) adj. 1. Ingenious, polite, gallant, good, elegant. 2. Witty, jocose, arch, comical, waggish.

ety: z̤arīf, q.v.+āna = Zend aff. āna or ana = S. अन
def: adj. = z̤arīf; — adv. Ingeniously, skilfully, &c.; wittily, facetiously.

ety: prob. a corr. of A. ضريح ẓarīḥ
def: s.f. (local) A railing or lattice-work surrounding a temple or tomb.
more: s. f. A railing or lattice-work surrounding a temple or tomb.

ety: Zend zairina = S. हरिण
def: adj. Golden; made of gold; — zarīna, s.m. Golden stuffs or goods
more: (from زر) adj. Golden. زرين مرغ zarrīṅ murg̠ẖ, s. m. 1. The name of a bird. 2. ( met. ) The sun.
subs: s.m. Name of a bird: (met.) the sun.

ety: Zend zairina = S. हरिण
def: adj. Golden; made of gold; — zarīna, s.m. Golden stuffs or goods
more: (from زر) adj. Golden; golden stuffs or goods.
subs: s.m. Name of a bird: (met.) the sun.

ety: inf. n. of ضرط
def: s.m. Breaking wind.

ety: inf. n. of زرق 'to cast (the eye
def: s.m. Turning (the eye) towards an object (so that scarce anything but the white appears); — hypocrisy; fraud, imposture; — detraction
more: s. m. 1. Turning the eyes. 2. (in Pers.) Hypocrisy, fraud. 3. Detraction. زرق برق zarḳ-barḳ, s. m. Glare, glitter, show, splendid cloths, splendour.
subs: s.m. Glare, glitter, show, magnificence, pomp, splendour; glittering or shining cloth, splendid clothes; — thunder and lightning.

def: adj. See bhaṛak-dār , under bhaṛak , 1. Thunder-and-lightning; ( Slang ) gaudy. 2. Glittering; splendid.

ety: prob. fr. Zend zairina or zaranya; S. हरिण or हिरण्य
def: s.m. Arsenic, orpiment (syn. hartāl).

ety: prob. fr. Zend zairina or zaranya; S. हरिण or हिरण्य
def: s.m. Arsenic, orpiment (syn. hartāl).

ety: prob. fr. Zend zairina or zaranya; S. हरिण or हिरण्य
def: s.m. Arsenic, orpiment (syn. hartāl).

ety: prob. fr. Zend zairina or zaranya; S. हरिण or हिरण्य
def: s.m. Arsenic, orpiment (syn. hartāl).
more: (or زرنه zarna or زرني zarnī ) s. m. Arsenick, orpiment. (Perhaps inverted from αρσενικον ).

def: s.m. = ẕarra, q.v. adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), A little; — a little while, short time; a slight or trivial matter; — adv. Just, would you just, please, kindly (e.g. ẕarā yěh uṭhā-denā)
subs: adv. Little by little, by degrees; every bit, every jot and tittle
adj. (f. -ī -sī), A little; slight, trivial, trifling; — s.m. Just a little
adv. In a little while, in a very short time, soon, quickly; — with much trouble or difficulty.

ety: inf. n. of ضرّ 'to harm,' &c.
def: s.m. = ẓarar, q.v. s.m. Harm, injury, hurt, mischief, damage; defect, deficiency, detriment, loss; — poverty; affliction, distress, anguish
more: (Heb. Strait, adversity, hostility) s. m. Loss, damage, injury, distress.
subs: (-ko), To cause harm to come (to), to harm, hurt, &c.

ety: i.q. ẕarra, q.v.
def: adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), A little; — a little while, short time; a slight or trivial matter; — adv. Just, would you just, please, kindly (e.g. ẕarā yěh uṭhā-denā)
more: adv. A little ( thoṛā , 1). zarā , intj. Please! Do! Would you just! 1. Zarā yeh uṭhā denā! Would you take this up, please! 2. Na mujh se karo aise tum chochle, Na lipṭo, zarā haṭ-ke baiṭho pare! Wom. Shauq. zarā zarā, zarā zarā kar-ke , adv. Every bit; every jot and tittle; inch by inch. zarā sā, zarā sī , adj. A little. zarā kī zarā , adv. For an instant. zarā meṅ zarā , Little out of little. zarā men zarā chīz unheṅ bhī de detī hūṅ . Mah. Wom.
subs: adv. Little by little, by degrees; every bit, every jot and tittle
adj. (f. -ī -sī), A little; slight, trivial, trifling; — s.m. Just a little
adv. In a little while, in a very short time, soon, quickly; — with much trouble or difficulty.
ex: ذرا دروازہ بند کر دیجیے
zarā darvāza band kar dījiye
Close the door, if you may
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian ذره (zarra), from Arabic ذَرَّة (ḏarra).

ety: for A. ذرة ẕarrat, n. of un. fr. ذرّ 'to sprinkle, scatter,' &c.
def: s.m. A mote, atom, particle, a little, jot, tittle, an ace (see ẕarrā or ẕarā); — adj. (f. -ī), Little
more: n. m. A particle; an atom; a mote.
(from ذرّ) s. m. An atom, a particle, a little, a jot, a tittle, an ace. (Plur. ذرات). ذره وار ẕarra-wār, Like an atom, minute, a little.
s. m. A mote. adj. Little.
subs: ẕarra-wār, adj. Like an atom, minute, a little; humble, lowly.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian ذره (zarra), from Arabic ذَرَّة (ḏarra).

def: (intens. n. fr. ضرب 'to beat,' &c.), s.m. lit. 'One who beats or strikes much'; a stamper (of coin), a coiner; a mint-master.
s.m. pl. (of ẕarra), Motes, atoms, particles, &c.

ety: dim. of ẕarra or ẕarrā, q.v.
def: s.f. A minute particle, an atom, a very little, &c.
more: (dimin. of ذره q. v. or fem.) An atom, a little, &c.

def: Zoroaster
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian زرتشت (zartušt).

ety: v.n. fr. ضر 'to harm,' &c.
def: adj. & adv. Necessary, needful, requisite, expedient; urgent; unavoidable, indispensable, essential, imperative, &c.; — urgently; certainly, assuredly, of course; without fail; absolutely; peremptorily
more: adj. ضر necessitated. 1. Necessary; urgent; important. 2. Essential; indispensable. 3. Incumbent; obligatory; imperative. zurūr, zarūr ; Rus. jarūr , adv. 1. Urgently. 2. Certainly; of course; without fail; absolutely; peremptorily. zurūr zurūr, zurūr hī, bil-zurūr, zurūratan , adv. 1. Urgently; indispensably. 2. Ex necessitate rei ; necessarily; of or by necessity; perforce. zurūr hai . It is imperative; he is bound to. jāë-zurūr , A privy; necessary. jī zurūr , adv. ( Ironic .) 1. Certainly! of course! 2. Really! you dont say so!
(from ضرّ) adj. Necessary, expedient, unavoidable; sub. The necessary.
subs: or ẓarūr-hī, or biʼẓ-ẓarūr, adv. Necessarily, of necessity, perforce; urgently; indispensably
or jā-ẓarūr, s.m. A necessary, a privy.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian ضرور (zurūr), from Arabic ضُرُور (ḍurūr).

ety: for A. ضرورة; see ẓarūr
def: s.f. Necessity, need, want, exigency; indigence; — compulsion, constraint, force, violence
more: n. f. Lack; necessity (احتياج). zurūrat-i-ashadd . Extreme or urgent necessity; emergency. zurūrat paṛnā yā h. , v. n. To have need of or occasion for. zurūrat kā vaqt . Time of need. zurūrat-mand , Ped. adj. Necessitous ( hājatmand ). zurūrat meṅ kām ānā , v. n. To serve one in time of need. ba-qadr i-zurūrat . As needed. zurūrī chiṭṭhī , n. f. An urgent letter or despatch. zurūrī kām . Urgent work. masārif-i-zurūrī . Necessary expenses.
s. f. 1. Force, violence, compulsion, constraint. 2. Want, indigence, necessity, exigence. ضرورتًا ẓarūrataṉ, or ضرورتا ẓarūratā, adv. Necessarily, of or by necessity.
subs: (-kī), Need (of a thing) to arise, to have need (of), to have occasion (for)
adj. Necessitous, needy, in want ( = ḥājat-mand)
(-ko), To prove useful (to one) in time of need, to serve (one) in time of need.

ety: for A. ضرورةً, acc. of ẓarūrat
def: adv. By necessity, of necessity, necessarily, perforce.
adj. Necessary, &c. ( = ẓarūr, q.v.); — s.f. (local), A necessity, a thing needful ( = ẓarūrat).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian ضروری (ẓarūrī), itself from Arabic ضَرُورِيّ (ḍarūriyy). By surface analysis, ضرور (ẓurūr) + ی (ī).

def: (for A. ضرورية, fem. of ضروري), adj. fem. = ẓarūrī, q.v. adj. Necessary, &c. ( = ẓarūr, q.v.); — s.f. (local), A necessity, a thing needful ( = ẓarūrat).
s.f. pl. (of ضرورية, fem. of ضروري), Necessary or indispensable things, necessaries, requisites.
more: n. f. pl. of ضروري Necessaries.
(plur. of ضروري) s. f. Necessaries, requisites.

def: bitterly
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian زار زار (zār-zār).

ety: fem. of ẕū, q.v.
def: s.f. Possessor, owner, mistress (in these senses used as a prefixed noun; and often better rendered 'having, possessed of, endowed with'); essence, substance, nature, radical constituent; soul; body, person, self (i.e. a man's self, or a thing's self); generation, breed, tribe, caste; genus, species; sort, kind (in these senses, prob. connected, through the Persian, with the S. जाति; H. jāt; which the Hindūstānīs commonly corrupt into ẕāt); (in Gram.) a real substantive, a substantive ( = ism-ě-ẕāt, as opp. to ism-ě-maʻnī, and to ṣifat)
more: n. f. ذات essence. 1. State or form of being; nature; constitution; essence; substance. Yeh kapṛā zāt kā achchhā haī . 2. The body; person; self. Mujhe āp kī zāt kā baṛā bharosā hai . 3. (Cor. of H. jāt ) Origin ( asl ); birth; caste ( jāt , 2). 1. Ab to rupayā zāt hai . Prov. Money now is caste. 2. Inkī pāṅchvīṅ zāt hai . He is of the Fifth caste. (He has no caste). 3. Roṭī gaī muṅh meṅ, zāṭ gaī gū meṅ . Wom. Prov. The bread went down his throat, his caste into a filthy moat. (Said of an interested mesalliance or religious conversion). 4. Hāth beche haiṅ, kuchh zāt nahīṅ bechī . Prov. I've sold my limbs to you, not my caste. 5. Damṛī kī hāṅḍī ṭūṭī, kutte kī zāt pahchānī . Prov. A farthing pot broken, the dog known by this token. 4. Kind; sort; character; nature. Is zāt kā kapṛā lāo! zāt se girā-huā , An outcaste. zāt se nikālnā , v. a. To put out of caste. zāt-i-sharīf , ( Ironic .) A c…

(from ذو Master, owner, possessor) s. f. (dakh. s. m.) 1. A mistress, possessor. 2. (Sans. जाति ) The essence, nature, soul, self, substance, property, the body, person, cast, race, breed. ذات الجنب ẕātu-l-jamb, s. m. The pleurisy. ذات بهائي ẕāt-bhāʼī (dakh.) A relative, kinsman. ذات جانا ẕāt jānā, v. n. To become an outcast. ذات دينا ẕāt denā, v. a. To give up oneʼs cast or relinquish the benefits of oneʼs religion by eating, &c. with a person of another sect: also, to give cast, or to receive into any sect as a member. ذات کي گن نکالنا ẕāt ke guṇ nikālnā (dakh. i. q. بدذاتي کرنا) To act wickedly or villainously. ذات لينا يا مارنا ẕāt-lenā, or -mārnā, v. a. To deprive another of cast, by touching his food, &c. &c.
subs: s.m.(?) Inflammation of the lungs, pneumonia, peri-pneumony
s.m. Inflammation in the chest; pleurisy; a collecting of matter in the chest
( = jāt jānā), To lose caste, become an outcast

(apnī), To give up (one's own) caste, or to relinquish the benefits of (one's) religion, by eating, &c. with a person of another caste; — to give caste (to, -ko), to receive (one) as a member of a caste or sect ( = jāt denā)
adj. & s.m. Out of caste; — an outcast
v.t. To put out of caste (see jāt)
s.m.f. An excellent mind or soul; a noble personage; (ironic.) a consummate knave
ẕāt mārnā (-kisī-kī), To deprive of caste (by touching the food, &c. &c. of another)
s.m. (Gram.) A generic noun, a common noun
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian ذات (zāt), from Arabic ذَات (ḏāt).

ety: for H. जटल; prob. S. जड+Prk. डी = S. ल+ई
def: s.f. Chattering, chatter, foolish prating, nonsense, false oridle stories, quibbling, tattle
more: (corrupt. from جٿل) s. f. Chattering, talking much and foolishly, quibbling, tattle. زٿل مارنا zaṭal mārnā, To chatter, to talk nonsense. زٿل هانکنا zaṭal hānknā, To quibble.
subs: s.m. One who talks nonsense; one who tells false and idle stories
zaṭal hāṅknā, v.n. To talk nonsense; to quibble; to tell false and idle stories.

def: n. f. Chatter; gup; nonsense. zaṭal uṛānā, mārnā, yā hāṅknā , v. a. To chatter; gup; talk nonsense.

ety: zaṭal, q.v.+Prk. इओ = S. इक (इन्+कः)
def: s.m. One who talks nonsense, &c. ( = zaṭal-bāz).
n. m. One who talks nonsense.

ety: rel. n. of ẕāt
def: adj. Essential, substantial, natural, constituent, original, innate, constitutional, real, material, intrinsic, fundamental; personal (cf. aṣlī, and jātī).
more: adj. Essential; constituent (اصلي 1, 3, 4.). zātī taٴalluq yā fāëdā . Personal interest or advantage. zātī muāmelāt , Personal affairs or circumstances. jauhar-i-zātī , Intrinsic worth or value. haisy'at-i-zātī . Individual capacity. rusūkh-i-zātī . Personal influence. qaid-i-zātī . Personal restraint.
adj. (Sans. जातीय ) Essential, natural, innate, intrinsick.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian ذَاتِی (zātī), borrowed from Arabic ذَاتِيّ (ḏātiyy), from ذَات (ḏāt, “self”) + ـِيّ (-iyy).

ety: prob. a local corr. of jātīlā = jāt+S. aff. इल+कः
def: adj. (f. -ī) = ẕātī, q.v. adj. Essential, substantial, natural, constituent, original, innate, constitutional, real, material, intrinsic, fundamental; personal (cf. aṣlī, and jātī).
n. m. lit . a pain in the side. Rheumatism of the intercostal muscles; pleurodynia.
Peripneumony; inflammation of the lungs; pneumonia.

ety: v.n. fr. زوج; ii زوج 'to couple,' &c.
def: s.m. A pair, couple; fellow, like; spouse, husband
more: n. m. 1. A couple; a pair. 2. A husband; spouse; consort.
(Chald. ) s. m. A couple, a pair, alike, a spouse, a husband.
subs: s.m. 'The husband of the virgin or of the godly woman,' a title of the Khalīfa ʻAlī.

ety: for A. روجة, fem. of zauj
def: s.f. A wife, spouse.
more: n. f. A wife ( jorū ). zauja-i-mutalliqa , n. f. A divorced wife.

ety: for A. زوجية
def: s.f. Marriage-state, marriage; — married woman, wite.
more: n. f. The married state; the state of coverture. haqq-i-zaujīyat . The rights of a Mah. married woman.

ety: inf. n. of ذاق 'to taste'
def: s.m. Taste, enjoyment, delight, joy, pleasure, voluptuousness
more: n. m. Taste (mental). zauq se, shauq se , adv. With pleasure; willingly.
s. m. Taste, delight, joy, pleasure, voluptuousness. ذوق سي ẕauḳ se, adv. With pleasure, willingly, with all my heart.
subs: s.m. Pleasure and delight, great pleasure
(se), To have a taste (for), take pleasure (in).
ex: اردو میں ذوق رکھنا
To have a taste in urdu
تمہارا گانے میں اچّھا ذوق رکھنا
You have a good taste in music
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian ذوق (zawq), from Arabic ذَوْق (ḏawq). Related to ذائقہ (zāyqā).

ety: inf. n. of زور 'to visit'
def: s.m. Visiting (a shrine, &c.), going on a pilgrimage (cf. ziyārat).
wety: From Classical Persian زور (zōr, “strength, force; power, might”).

def: s.m. pl. (of ẓābit̤a, q.v.), Rules, canons, laws, &c.

ety: prob. corr. of A. azwād, pl. of zād, q.v.
def: s.m. Provision for a journey (in P. also zawāda).
s.f. pl. (of زاهرة, fem. of zāhir, act. part. of زهر 'to shine'), Beautiful flowers; flowers; ornaments.
s. plur. (of زايده) Things additional, additions.
s.m.f. pl. (of زائِده or رائِدة, fem. of zāʼid, q.v.), Things additional, additions, superfluities, redundancies; excrescences; (in Gram.) letters of increase, servile letters.
n. pl. of زايد more. Additions; augmentations; superfluities.
s.f. pl. (of زاجرة zājirat; see zajr), Exhortations, chiding or restraining speeches; prohibitions, forbidden things
more: plur. (of زجر or زاجرة) Prohibition, forbidden things. زواجرشرعي zawājir-i-shar-īﻌ, Prohibited by law.

ety: inf. n. of زول 'to pass away,' &c.
def: s.m. Declining (as the sun from the meridian); declination; setting (of the sun, &c.); decline, wane, decay; fall; cessation; defect, deficiency, failure; harm, loss, injury (syn. nuqṣān); humiliation; misery, wretchedness
more: n. m. زيل putting to a distance, removing. 1. Fall; failure; declination; decay (اتَار 3.). Har kamāle rā zavāle . P. Prov. All maturity is destined to decay. 2. Declination of the sun. zavāl kā vaqt , + Mah. Wom.; H. tīsre pahar The afternoon. Zāvālkā vaqt hai, bachche ko bāhar na jānedo ! Wom. It is the afternoon, dont let the child go outside! (Evil spirits are abroad at this time). zavāl-i-māh . The moon's wane, from full to new moon.
(from زول) s. m. Decline, wane, declination, cessation; misery, wretchedness, setting of the sun. زوال پذير zawāl-paẕīr, adj. Transitory, fading.
subs: adj. Liable to decay, perishable, evanescent, transitory, fading
s.m. The time of the sun's decline, the afternoon

def: s.m. pl. (of ẕanab), Tails.

ety: accus. of ẕū; like which it is used
def: adj. Powerful ( = ẕuʼl-qadr)
more: (plur. in obj. case of ذو ẕū, q. v.) Having, possessing, endowed with.

ety: for A. زاوية, v.n. fr. زوي 'to draw (a thing

r: ze, zāʼe mǒhmala, zāʼe manqūt̤a, z., j, roj, roz, jor, zor, abjad
def: (called zāʼe mǒhmala or zāʼe manqūt̤a) is the sixteenth letter of the Hindūstānī alphabet (the thirteenth of the Persian, and the eleventh of the Arabic), and has the sound of the English z. It has no corresponding letter in the Nāgarī alphabet, in which it is represented by ज़, or more commonly changed to ज j, as roj for roz, jor for zor, &c. In reckoning by abjad, it stands for 7; and in almanacs, it represents Saturday, and the sign Scorpio.
more: called zāë mo'jamā or zāë manqūta is the 11th letter of the Arabic, the 13th of the Persian, and the 6th of the Hindustānī alphabet. It has no corresponding letter in the Sanskrit alphabet, and ज़ is substituted to represent it in the Nāgrī character. Its sound is that of the English z. In the notation of numbers by the Arabic letters it stands for seven. In almanacs it represents Saturday, and the sign Scorpio. In Persian poetry it is used by way of contraction for az from, by, etc., as za-bas for az-bas , in abundance. It is interchangable as an initial, medial, and final with j , as P. zor =H. jor power; P. zamāna =H. jamāna time; P. tarzamān =P. tarjamān translator; P. kazāvah =P. kajāvah a camel's saddle; P. tezī =H. tejī sharpness; P. roz =P. roj day; P. arz =P. arj price; P. jahez =H. dahej dowry; P. āvāz =H. avāj sound. As a final it is interchangable with s , as Hurmuz = Hurmus ;…

is the eleventh letter of the Arabic, and the thirteenth of the Persian alphabet. It has no corresponding letter in the Sanskrit alphabet; and, ज ( j ) is substituted to represent it in the Nāgarī characters. Its sound is that of the English z. In the denotation of numbers by the Arabic letters, it stands for seven. In almanacks it represents Saturday, and the sign Scorpio. In poetry it is used by way of contraction for از From, by, &c. as زبس zi-bas, In abundance, although. It is changed (1) into ج as روج for روز. (2) into س as اياس for اياز A personʼs name. (3) into غ as گريغ for گريز. It is substituted (1) for چ as پزشک for پچشک A physician. (2) for ت in Arabic verbs of the form افتعال when ز is the first letter of the root; as, ازتان from زينت is changed to ازدان and that to ازّان. (3) For خ in the aorist of Persian verbs which have خ before the final تن of the infinitive, as آويز fr…

r: zhe, zāʼe fārsī, zāʼe ʻajamī, z, s, pleasure, azure, j
def: (called zāʼe fārsī, or zāʼe ʻajamī, 'the Persian z'), is the seventeenth letter of the Hindūstānī and the fourteenth of the Persian alphabet. It has the sound of s in the English pleasure, or z in azure. It has no corresponding character in the Devanāgarī and Arabic alphabets. It is often confounded with z, and occasionally changed to j.
more: the fourteenth letter of the Persian alphabet, has no corresponding character in the Arabic or Sanskrit. Its sound is that of s in the English word pleasure; or, of the French g or j in gens, jour: and, that sound is here denoted by zh. This letter is sometimes changed to ج as پجمرده pajmurda, for پژمرده pazhmurda.

ety: arabicized from P. zih, 'a bow-string,' q.v.
def: s.m. A builder's string or line, a mason's rule; — a set of astronomical tables; an astronomical almanac.
more: s. m. Astronomical tables, a masonʼs rule.

ety: inf. n. of ضيق 'to be or become narrow or strait'
def: s.m. Straitness of mind, mental distress, vexation, anxiety, anguish, affliction, oppression of spirit, melancholy, doubt, anything that tortures the mind, hippishness; difficulty, dilemma
more: n. f. ضيق made narrow. 1. Depression of spirits; distress. 2. Difficulty; dilemma. zīq-ul-nafs ; H. sāṅs-rog , n. m. 1. Difficulty of breathing. 2. Asthma.

(Heb. ) s. m. Anguish, vexation, melancholy, affliction, anxiety, doubt, oppression of spirit, or any thing that tortures the mind, hippishness. ضيق النفس ẓīḳu-n-nafas, s. m. The asthma. ¶ ẓaiḳ or ẓaiyiḳ, adj. Contracted, narrow, straitened.
subs: s.m. Difficulty of breathing; the asthma; shortness of breath.

def: adj. See ازخود رفته az ḵẖụd rafta, under از az.
s. f. See ازخود رفتگي az-ḵẖụd-raftagī, under از az.

ety: inf. n. of ذکر 'to remember; to mention,' &c.
def: s.m. Remembering, remembrance; memory; commemoration; — mention, telling, relating, relation, recital, report, account; praise, eulogy, fame; the praise and glorification of God (the saying subhānaʼl-lāh, and al-ḥamdǒ-liʼl-lāh, and allāhǒ akbar, and lā ilāha illaʼl-lāhǒ, and other forms of praise); thanking God; prayer, supplication; a reading or reciting of the Qǒrʼān
more: n. m. 1. Talk; mention; report; recital; account. 1. Usī kā zikr, usī kā fikr . 2. Kāl sunā thā maiṅ ne jikrā, panghaṭ kī panihār suṅī, Udhal paṛāṅ hotā āyā saë, thārī kulyan sārī sūṅ . Rus. Wom. I heard it at the well, the women drawing water tell, How all your kith and kin have noted been for wanton sin! 2. Taking or repeating the name of God. 3. An invocation; prayer. zikr-i-khair , 1. Reading of the Qurān and recital of the praises and names of God; preaching. 2. Good news. zikr k. , v. a. To tell; relate; state ( barnan k .). zikr mazkūr , n. m. Talk; conversation; allusion.

(Heb. Chal. ) s. m. 1. Remembrance, memory, commemoration, mention. 2. Praise, fame, recital, relation. 3. Reading of the ḳurʼān and recital of the praise and names of God. ذکرکرنا ẕikr karnā, v. a. To recite, mention, relate, express, &c.
subs: (-kā), To make mention (of), to mention, tell (of), relate, express, state, &c.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian ذکر (zakar), from Arabic ذَكَر (ḏakar).

def: remembrance of God (often in one's heart)
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian ذِکْرِ اِلٰهِی (zikr-i ilāhī), a calque of Arabic ذِكْرِ الله (ḏikri llāh). By surface analysis, ذِکْر (zikr, “mention; remembrance”) + اِلٰہی (ilāhī, “divine”).

def: a tribute, commemoration, good word; a backhanded compliment
wety: From ذِکْر (zikr, “mention”) + ِ (-i) + خَیر (xair, “good; well”).

def: s. m. Shortness of breath.

ety: v.n. fr. ضلع 'to incline, decline,' &c.
def: s.m. (but com. f. in Ar.), Side (of a geom. figure, &c.); part; a division (of a province), a district; column (in the page of a book); — the art of speaking with double meaning so that the chain of both senses be uninterrupted through the discourse (syn. talāzum-ě-kalām), a kind of punning
more: n. m. Pl. azlā . 1. A division of a province; a district; a tract of country constituting the jurisdiction of a judge and collector, or, in Non-Regulation Provinces of a Deputy Commissioner. 2. A column (of a page). 3. ( Geom .) A side ( bhujā ). 4. A kind of punning ( riٴaāyat , 5). zila-bandī , n. f. The division of a province into zillas or districts. zila bolnā , v. n. To cap zilās (puns). zila jis meṅ band o bast ho gayā ho . A settled district.

(Heb. ) s. m. 1. A side, part. 2. A district, a division. 3. A column (in the page of a book). 4. (in Hindūstān) The art of speaking with double meaning, so that the chain of both senses be uninterrupted through the discourse. (This is called in Persian and Arabic تلازم کلام).
subs: s.f. The division of a province into ẓilaʻs or districts

vulg. ẓileʻ-dār, s.m. An officer who has the charge of the revenues of a ẓilaʻ, superintendent of a district; an officer who makes advances to cultivators and collects rents, &c.; an officer (in the canal-department) who supervises measurements and the distribution of water
vulg. ẓileʻ-dārī, s.f. The office or situation of ẓilaʻ-dār.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian ضلع (zil', “side, edge”), from Arabic ضِلْع (ḍilʕ, “side, edge, division”). Compare Nepali जिल्ला (jillā) and Marathi जिल्हा (jilhā).

ety: = zīr
def: s.f. A shrill note, acute tone; the treble (in music).
more: s. f. See زير zīr, The treble, &c.

def: direct plural; oblique/vocative singular

n. m. 1. A district superintendent. 2. An officer who makes advances to cultivators and collects rents, etc; a Canal Officer. 3. An officer in the Canal Department who supervises measurements and the distribution of water.
n. f. The office of a zile-dār .

ety: v.n. (fr. ظلّ 'to be or become shady,' &c.
def: s.m. Shade, shadow
more: (Ch. ) s. m. Shadow. ظل الله z̤illu-l-lāh, or ظل سبحاني z̤ill-i-subʼḥānī, Shadow of God; ( met. ) A king, an emperour. دام ظله or ظلـﮩم dāma z̤illuhu or z̤illuhum, May his or their prosperity continue!
subs: or z̤ill-ě-subḥānī, 'The shadow of God'; (met.) a king, an emperor
(or -hum), 'May his (or their) shadow last long, may his (or their) prosperity continue.

ety: inf. n. of ذلّ 'to be easy,' &c.
def: s.m. Ease, easiness, gentleness; tractableness, submissiveness.

ety: for A. ذلة, inf. n. of ذلّ 'to be base,' &c.
def: s.f. Baseness, meanness, vileness, abjectness, contemptibleness, abasement, humiliation, dishonour, disgrace, indignity, affront, insult; — ẕillat denā (-ko), To dishonour, disgrace, &c. ( = ẕalīl karnā, q.v.)
more: n. f. ذل was vile. Insult; indignity; disgrace.
(from ذلّ) s. f. Baseness, vileness, meanness, contemptibleness, abjectness, dishonour, abasement, affront.
subs: (-se, or kisī-ke hāth-se), To suffer dishonour, or disgrace, or shame, &c. (at the hands of).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian ذِلَّت (zillat), from Arabic ذِلَّة (ḏilla).

def: s.f. incor. for zallat, 'error,' &c., q.v.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian ذِلَّت (zillat), from Arabic ذِلَّة (ḏilla).

def: oblique plural of ضِلَع (zila', “district”)
(v.n. fr. ضمد 'to bind,' &c.), s.f.(m.?) A bandage, fillet, kerchief (bound upon a wound, &c.); dressing, poultice, plaster; ointment; liniment; embrocation.
more: n. m. A liniment; embrocation; ointment.
(prop. ẓimād ) s. f. 1. A bandage or fillet for binding the head or a wound. 2. A plaster, an embrocation, cataplasm.

def: (v.n. fr. زمّ 'to tie,' &c.), s.f.(m. ?), Nose-rein, nose-string (of a camel, &c.); rein, bridle.
more: (prop. zimām, from زمّ Heb. and Ch. To bind, to rein) s. f. m. A rein, a bridle.

ety: zima+stān; Pārsī dameśtān; Pehl. himśtān; Zend zima+śtāna; S. हिम+स्थान
def: s.m. Winter; — wisdom, science.
more: (زم Cold, and استان Place) s. m. 1. Winter. 2. Wisdom.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian زمستان (zamistān).

ety: for A. ذمة ẕimmat, v.n. fr. ذم 'to blame'
def: s.m. Obligation, responsibility, suretyship, security; trust, charge, subjection; burden (as of proof); duty, service, fidelity; lineage; — ẕimme, postpn. In the suretyship or security (of, -ke); in charge (of)
more: n. m. ذم a compact. Charge; trust; responsibility. zimma-i-subūt, Onus probandī ; burden of proof. zimme k. , v. a. To entrust; deliver in trust; deposit with; give in one's charge. zimmah k. yā lenā, zimme-vār h. , v. a. To undertake a charge; take on oneself; to bail. zimmeh-vār, zimmeh-dār , adj. Responsible; answerable; liable. zimmeh-vār, zimmeh-dār , n. m. Guarantor; trustee; assignee. zimmeh-vārī, zimmeh-dārī , n. f. 1. Trust; charge; custody. 2. Engagement; guarantee; obligation. 3. Responsibility; liability. zimmeh-vārī se barī k . To discharge from zimmeh-vārī qānūnī . Legal liability.
s. m. Trust, charge, obligation, subjection, duty, service, fidelity, lineage. ذمي لينا ẕimme lenā, To take in charge, to take care of, to take upon oneʼs self.
subs: vulg. ẕimme-dār, adj. & s.m. Responsible, answerable, liable; — a responsible person, surety, security; guarantor, trustee, assignee
vulg. ẕimme-dārī, s.f. Engagement, obligation, guarantee; responsibility, liability; trust, charge, custody
(-ke), To give into the charge (of), make over (to), to deliver in trust, to deposit (with)
or ẕimme lenā (apne), To take in charge, undertake the charge (of), take upon oneself, become responsible for, be security or surety for
wety: From Arabic ذِمَّة (ḏimma).

ety: v.n. fr. ضمن 'to be responsible for,' &c.
def: s.m. Cover, fold, or envelope (of a letter); endorsement (of a grant, &c.); clause, section (in legal regulations, &c.); — contents, enclosure, anything included, or comprehended, or contained, or inserted; subject; idea, conception; — obligation, suretiship; — aid, favour
more: n. m. 1. A clause; section; article. 2. Contents; body. Zimn pahla . Section first. zimn k . Ped. v. a. To include; take in; comprise. zimn meṅ , adv. 1. In the course of; by the way.
s. m. 1. The cover of a letter. 2. The contents, any thing comprehended, contained or inserted, subject, an idea, conception. 3. Suretiship, an obligation. 4. Aid, favour.
subs: v.t. To enclose; to include, comprehend, comprise, &c.
adv. In the fold (of, -ke), in the contents (of), enclosed in, included in; in the course (of), by the way.

ety: acc. of ẓimn
def: adv. Inclusively, comprehensively, by implication, involved therein; intimately; tacitly; by the bye, by the way; into the bargain; — by way of surety or security; by way of assistance.
more: adv. By implication; by the way; indirectly. 2. By way of security.
adv. Comprehensively, by implication, tacitly, by the by, by the way.

def: by-election

ety: rel. n. fr. ẓimn
def: adj. Implicative; collateral, corroborative (as evidence).

ety: Pehl. zīn; Zend zaini
def: s.m. Saddle
more: n. m. A saddle. zīn-posh , n. m. A saddle cloth; housing. zīn-sāz , n. m. A saddler. zīn-savārī , n. f. Riding on horseback. zīn kasnā, yā kheṅchnā , v. n. To saddle (a horse). bibyānah zīn , n. m. A side saddle.
s. m. A saddle. زين باندهنا zīn bāndhnā, To saddle. زين پوش zīn-posh, s. m. A cloth fastened over the saddle, housing. زين گر zīn-gar, s. m. A saddler.
subs: s.m. A cloth fastened over the saddle, saddle-cloth, housing
zīn khaiṅćnā (-kā, or -par), To saddle (a horse, &c.)

ety: inf. n. of زني 'to commit fornication,' &c.
def: s.f. Fornication; adultery
more: n. f. Adultery (حرام). zinā bij-jabr , n. m. An indecent assault; rape.
(from Heb. To fornicate) s. f. Adultery, fornication. زنا زاده zinā-zāda, A bastard. زنا کار zinā-kār, s. m. An adulterer, a fornicator. زنا کاري zinākār-ī, s. f. Fornication, adultery, harlotry.
subs: s.f. Fornication, adultery, harlotry, illicit intercourse
wety: From Classical Persian زنا (zinā), from Arabic زِنَاء (zināʔ).

ety: prob. akin to S. श्रेणि
def: s.m. Ladder, stairs, steps
more: n. m. A ladder; stairs; steps. zīne ke ḍanḍe . The rungs of a ladder.
s. m. A ladder, stairs, steps.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian زِینَه (zīna).

def: n. m. An adulterer; a fornicator.

n. f. Adultery; illicit intercourse; criminal conversation. zinā-kārī bā kheshāṅ , n. f. Incest; sexual intercourse between persons who by reason of consanguinity or affinity cannot lawfully be united.

ety: for A. زينة, v.n. fr. زين 'to adorn'
def: s.f. Ornament, decoration, ornature, embellishment, dress; grace, beauty, elegance
more: n. f. زين Beauty; elegance ( zebāish ). Nahīṅ hājat hai zīnat kī jise khūbī Khudā ne dī, Ki jaise khush-numā lagtā hai dekho chāṅd bin gahne . Mīr Amman. Whom God with beauty hath endowed no ornament doth need, The beauteous moon though unadorned doth admiration breed.
(from زين) s. f. Ornament, beauty, elegance, dress, decoration.
subs: (with acc. of person or member, and abl. of thing), To adorn, beautify, &c.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian زِینَت (zīnat), borrowed from Arabic زِينَة (zīna). First attested in c. 1635 as Middle Hindi زینت (zynt /⁠zīnat⁠/).

def: direct plural of زِینَت (zīnat)
oblique plural of زِینَت (zīnat)
vocative plural of زِینَت (zīnat)

ety: fr. zīstan; rt. Zend ji, or jīv; S. जीवन्त, rt. जीव्
def: adj. Living, alive (syn. jītā)
more: adj. Alive; living ( jītā ). 1. Āp zindah jahān zindah . Prov. Self alive, the world alive. 2. Murdah ba-dast-i-zindah . P. Prov. The dead are at the mercy of the living. zindah-dil , adj. See khush-tabā . zindah-dilī , n. f. 1. Gaiety; cheerfulness. 2. See khush-tabٴaī. zindah-dar-gor , One foot in the grave. zindah rahnā , v. n. To live; survive ( jīte rahnā ). zindah k. , v. a. To bring to life.
subs: adj. & s.m. lit. 'Live-hearted'; pious, devout, holy; — lively, cheerful, gay, witty; — a devout or holy man, &c.
s.f. Piety; — cheerfulness, liveliness, gaiety; wittiness
v.t. To bring to life, to restore to life, to put life into.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian زِنْدَه (zinda). First attested in c. 1611 as Middle Hindi زندہ (zndh /⁠zinda⁠/).

def: long live
ex: ہندوستان زنده باد!
hindūstān zinda bād!
Long live India!
پاکستان زنده باد!
pākistan zinda bād!
Long live Pakistan!
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian زنده باد (zinda bād).

def: adj. Lively, gay, brisk.

ety: fr. zinda, q.v.
def: s.f. Life, living; sustenance, livelihood.
more: s. f. Life, living, existence, livelihood.

ety: zindak = zinda, q.v.+Zend aff. i = S. इ
def: s.f. Life, living, existence
more: n. f. Life (حيات). 1. Zindgī apnī gar is taur se guzrī, Gālib, Ham bhī kyā yād kareṅge ki Khudā rakhte the ? Gālib. Gālib! if thus your life's to pass, deprived of meat and drink, Must you too then his Providence confess, just you bethink? 2. Zindgī zindā-dilī kā hai nām, Murdāh-dil khāk jīyā karte haiṅ ! Zauq. A merry heart life you may call, But dullness is no life at all. zindgī talkh h. , To be sick or weary of life. zindgī se tang ānā , v. n. To be tired of life. zindgī meṅ , adv. In one's lifetime ( jīte jī ).
subs: To be weary or sick of life
ex: زندگی ختم بھی ہو جائے اگر نہ کبھی ختم الفت کا سفر
zindagī xatm bhī ho jāe agar na kabhī xatm ulfat kā safar
Even if life comes to an end, may the journey of love never come to an end.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian زِنْدَگِی (zindagī). First attested in c. 1625 as Middle Hindi زندگی (zindagī).

def: direct plural of زِنْدَگِی (zindagī, “life”)
oblique plural of زِنْدَگِی (zindagī, “life”)
vocative plural of زِنْدَگِی (zindagī, “life”)

ety: Pehl. zand; Zend zañtu; S. जन्तु
def: s.m. Dungeon; prison, gaol.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian زِنْدَان (zindān). First attested in c. 1649 as Middle Hindi زندان (zndan /⁠zindān⁠/).

def: n. m. A prison ( jel-khāna ).
adj. & s.m. Of a prison; imprisoned; — prisoner, captive.
oblique plural of زِنْدَان (zindān, “prison, jail”)
vocative plural of زِنْدَان (zindān, “prison, jail”)

ety: fr. P. zandīk, fr. zand
def: s.m. A magian, fire-worshipper; — one of a bad religion, an infidel, atheist.
more: s. A follower of Zoroaster; one of a bad religion, a magian.

def: s.m. Care, caution; protection, defence, patronage; — intj. Take care! beware! mind! — adv. (followed by neg. na), By no means, on no account; never.
intj. = زنہار zinhār, q.v. s.m. Care, caution; protection, defence, patronage; — intj. Take care! beware! mind! — adv. (followed by neg. na), By no means, on no account; never.
adv. By no means; never.
see زينـہ‍ار zīnhār.
1. s. m. Care, caution, protection, defence, patronage. 2. interj. Take care, beware. adv. By no means.
s.f. Fringe, border, edging.

ety: for जिड़ = जड
def: s.f. = zaṭal, q.v. s.f. Chattering, chatter, foolish prating, nonsense, false oridle stories, quibbling, tattle
subs: s.m. One who talks nonsense; one who tells false and idle stories
zaṭal hāṅknā, v.n. To talk nonsense; to quibble; to tell false and idle stories.

ety: = zer, q.v.
def: s.f. A fine, soft, or low sound; acute tone; under-tone; — the smallest string (of a lute, &c.); the treble (in music)
more: n. f. 1. The shortest string in a lute, etc. 2. A fine soft sound; the treble. zīr o bam . 1. A pair of small kettledrums. 2. Low and high notes.
s. f. A fine soft sound, the smallest string of a lute, &c., the treble (in musick).
subs: adv. With a soft or low sound, softly
Low and high notes; — a pair of small kettle-drums.

def: s.m. = زيره zīra, q.v. (v.n. fr. ذرع 'to stretch forth the arm'), s.m. lit. 'The arm from the elbow to the tip of the middle finger'; a cubit; a yard; — the two bright stars α and β in the heads of Gemini; the seventh mansion of the moon.

ety: = S. जीर
def: s.m. Cummin-seed; — pollen (of a flower)
more: n. m. S. जीर to digest. 1. Commin-seed. 2. Pollen. zīra-safaid , n. m. Cuminum cyminum . Wat. zīra-i-siyāh , n. m. Carraway seeds.
subs: s.m. Cummin-water, solution of cummin-seeds; a mixture of ground cummin-seed, dry ginger, mint, assafœtida, dried green mangoes, salt, and chillies, dissolved in water (taken as a digestive).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian زیره (zīra).

def: (v.n. fr. ذرع 'to stretch forth the arm'), s.m. lit. 'The arm from the elbow to the tip of the middle finger'; a cubit; a yard; — the two bright stars α and β in the heads of Gemini; the seventh mansion of the moon.
more: s. m. 1. A yard. 2. A cubit. 3. Two bright stars in the head of Gemini.

ety: for A. زراعة, inf. n. of زرع 'to sow,' &c.
def: s.f. Sowing, tilling; tillage, husbandry, agriculture; a sown or cultivated field, sown land; a standing crop
more: n. f. زرع he sowed. 1. Tillage ( bo-jot ). 2. A cultivated farm or field; sown land; the standing crop. zirāat-pesha ; H. kisān , An agriculturist. zirāٴat k .; H. khetī k. , v. a. To cultivate; grow crops; to reclaim (land).
(from زرع Heb. To scatter seed, to sow) s. f. Agriculture, husbandry, sowing, sown field, sown land, crop.
subs: s.m. One whose business or occupation is sowing or tilling, husbandman, agriculturist
(-kī), To till, to cultivate; to grow crops; to reclaim (land).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian زِرَاعَت (zirā'at), from Arabic زِرَاعَة (zirāʕa).

ety: inf. n. of ضارب, iii of ضرب 'to strike,' &c.
def: s.m. Contending, fighting, fencing; conquering; — (inf. n. of ضرب 'to cover' (a camel)), coitus.
more: (from ضرب) 1. s. m. Fighting, fencing, conquering. 2. Coitus. ¶ ẓarrāb, A coiner, a mint-master.

def: giraffe
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian زَرَافَه (zarāfa), influenced by English giraffe. Doublet of جَراف (jarāf).

ety: Pārsī zreh; Pehl. zrāe; Zend zrādha, fr zrād = S. ह्राद्
def: s.f. Iron armour made with rings, chain-armour
more: n. m. Chain armour. zirah-posh . A man in armour.
(prop. zirih ) s. f. Iron armour made with rings. زره پوش zirah-posh, A man in armour.
subs: adj. & s.m. Clothed in chain-armour, mailed; — a man clad in armour

ety: prob. zer or zīr, q.v.+ak = S. अक
def: adj. Acute, shrewd, sharp, quick, smart, quick in understanding, ingenious, intelligent, sagacious, penetrating.
more: adj. Ingenious, intelligent, sagacious, penetrating, acute, shrewd.

def: s.f. Acuteness, acumen, quickness, ingenuity, intelligence, discernment, sagacity.
more: s. f. Ingenuity, sagacity, acumen, acuteness, discernment.

r: zīrā-ki, zī, az, ra, rāʼe?, ki
ety: zī = az+ra = rāʼe?+ki
def: conj. For the reason that, because that, because, since, on account of.
barberry
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian زرشک (zirišk).

def: s.m. = zaṭallī, q.v. s.m. One who talks nonsense, &c. ( = zaṭal-bāz).

ety: for A. ذروة, fr. ذرو or ذري 'to be raised,' &c.
def: s.f. Top of a camel's hump; summit (of a mountain, &c.), pinnacle; — much wealth, opulence (cf. s̤arwat); — majesty, pomp.
more: s. f. 1. The summit, pinnacle. 2. Majesty, pomp.

ety: Pārsī zist; Zend zoizhda, fr. zizhdā, rt. zish; cf. S. जिष्
def: adj. Ugly, unseemly, hideous; deformed; inhuman; obscene, filthy
more: adj. Deformed, ugly, inhuman, hideous. زشت خو zisht-ḵẖū, Of ugly habits or disposition. زشت رو zisht-rū, adj. Ugly.
subs: adj. Of ugly habits; of a wicked disposition; bad-tempered

def: (in Arabic) z̤ā (called z̤ā-ě-mǒʻjama or z̤ā-ě-manqūt̤a), the twenty-third letter of the Urdū alphabet (the seventeenth of the Arabic, from which it is taken). It occurs only in words borrowed from the Arabic, or in the secondary (Persian and Hindūstānī) formations from such words. By the Arabs it is usually pronounced like a strongly articulated palatal z, or (by some) like ض ẓ; but the Persians and the Mohammadans of Hindūstān give it the sound of ز z. As a numeral it denotes 900.
more: called zāë mojama or zāë manqūtā , the 17th letter of the Alphabet. It does not occur in pure Persian, nor has it any corresponding character in the Sanskrit or Hindī alphabet. In abjad it stands for 900.

the seventeenth letter of the Arabic alphabet, does not occur in pure Persian, nor has it any corresponding character in the Sanskrit alphabet. In the notation of numbers by the Arabic letters, it stands for 900. It is one of the radical letters; and, when it commences a verbal root, the servile ت in the measure تفعّل or تفاعل or افتعال may be changed into ظ and joined with the radical, in such wise as has been described, under ط, to be allowed with respect to that letter.
